summary: Moody's warns that federal transfers to Mexican states are losing momentum, growing just 0.9% annually between January and May, as scholarship and pension programs squeeze the finance ministry's budget. Slower revenue sharing tightens the fiscal room states have to spend, even as welfare commitments keep expanding.

lead.h1: Moody's warns on falling federal transfers to states as welfare spending rises
lead.context: Federal revenue shared with states grew just 0.9% annually between January and May, even as scholarship and pension programs squeeze the finance ministry's budget.
lead.background: The Recaudación Federal Participable (RFP) is the pool of federal tax revenue Mexico shares with states and municipalities, and welfare programs like scholarships and pensions draw on the same federal budget.
lead.drivers: Moody's Local points to slowing national growth and the resulting slowdown in RFP collection, while spending on social transfers keeps rising and squeezing Hacienda's operating budget.
lead.implications: Moody's says the mismatch between slowing revenue and rising operating spending could shrink operating margins for states and municipalities by the end of 2026.

coverage.title: Moody's warns on falling federal transfers to states as welfare spending rises
coverage.summary: Federal revenue shared with states grew just 0.9% annually between January and May, even as scholarship and pension programs squeeze the finance ministry's budget.

standing.text: The peso trades at 17.40 pesos to the dollar; inflation is 3.37%; the policy rate is 6.50%.
